picks.rationale: The Pistons, as the #1 seed, are playing at home in a decisive Game 7, a scenario where home teams historically perform well. Orlando is severely shorthanded with Franz Wagner officially out and Jonathan Isaac doubtful, significantly impacting their scoring and defense. The Magic's offense completely collapsed in Game 6, scoring only 19 points in the second half, and they haven't won since Wagner's injury. Detroit has strong momentum, winning the last two games to force this Game 7, and their top player, Cade Cunningham, is performing exceptionally well.

Game 7s are typically characterized by heightened defensive intensity and lower scoring, and this series has already seen low-scoring affairs. The Magic's offensive struggles are pronounced, especially without key players like Franz Wagner, as evidenced by their 79-point performance in Game 6. Detroit boasts a strong defense, ranking 2nd in defensive rating during the regular season. Two of the last three head-to-head matchups in this series have gone under the verified total, indicating a trend towards lower-scoring games.

The Cleveland Cavaliers are at full strength for this pivotal Game 7, with Donovan Mitchell and Evan Mobley cleared to play. In contrast, the Toronto Raptors are significantly depleted, with starting point guard Immanuel Quickley out for the series and All-Star Brandon Ingram questionable due to heel inflammation. The home team has been dominant in this series, holding a perfect 6-0 record, and the Cavaliers will benefit from playing in front of their home crowd. Cleveland also demonstrates superior three-point shooting at home in the playoffs, averaging 43.5% from beyond the arc.

This Game 7 is expected to be a defensive battle, a common characteristic of elimination games due to increased pressure and intensity. The Raptors' offensive output is likely to be hampered by the absence of Immanuel Quickley and the questionable status of Brandon Ingram, two key offensive contributors. Two of the last five head-to-head matchups in this series have already gone under the verified total, suggesting a potential for lower scores. The Cavaliers' strong defense and the Raptors' injury woes point towards a game where points will be at a premium.

The Cleveland Cavaliers are playing at home in a Game 7, where they have been undefeated (3-0) in this series and hold a strong 30-14 home record in the regular season. They enter this decisive game with a fully healthy roster, including key players Donovan Mitchell and Evan Mobley. The Toronto Raptors, however, are significantly hobbled by injuries, with Immanuel Quickley out for the series and Brandon Ingram questionable due to a heel injury. Given Cleveland's home dominance, their health advantage, and Toronto's key absences, the Cavaliers are strongly positioned to win this game outright.

picks.game: Montreal Canadiens vs Tampa Bay Lightning
picks.rationale: This is a pivotal Game 7, and the series has seen tight, low-scoring affairs, with 5 of the last 6 head-to-head playoff games going Under the total. While Game 6 ended 1-0 in overtime, both teams generated over seven Expected Goals, suggesting a potential for more offense, but playoff Game 7s often feature conservative play and strong goaltending. The Lightning's Andrei Vasilevskiy boasts a .929 save percentage and 1.78 goals-against average in his past six home playoff starts against Montreal, reinforcing the expectation of a low-scoring contest. The heavily juiced Under 5.5 line at -170 further indicates sharp money is backing a defensive battle.

The Colorado Avalanche enter Game 1 of the Second Round in dominant form, boasting a 9-0-1 record in their last 10 games and a strong 55-16-11 overall record. The Minnesota Wild are significantly handicapped by injuries, with defenseman Jonas Brodin officially OUT for Game 1 due to a lower-body injury, and key forward Joel Eriksson Ek listed as QUESTIONABLE with a lower-body injury. Brodin's absence weakens their defensive core, and Eriksson Ek's potential absence impacts their offense and face-off strength. With home-ice advantage and a healthier, in-form squad, Colorado is well-positioned to take Game 1.

picks.game: Montreal Canadiens vs Tampa Bay Lightning
picks.rationale: The Montreal Canadiens have consistently performed well against the spread in this matchup, covering the puckline in 7 of their last 7 head-to-head games against the Lightning. Conversely, the Tampa Bay Lightning have struggled to cover the -1.5 puckline, failing to do so in their last 10 games. In a high-stakes Game 7, games are often decided by a single goal, making the +1.5 goal cushion highly valuable. Montreal has also shown they can win in Tampa, taking two of three games there this series.

The Minnesota Wild are dealing with significant defensive injuries, as Jonas Brodin is out and Joel Eriksson Ek is questionable, which will likely compromise their defensive structure and penalty kill against a potent Avalanche offense. The Colorado Avalanche are a high-scoring team, and with Minnesota's defensive depth challenged, they should find ample scoring opportunities. The Wild also showed offensive capability by winning their last game 5-2. The stability of the Over 5.5 line at -132 suggests that despite the playoff intensity, a higher-scoring game is anticipated.

Both starting pitchers, Merrill Kelly (9.20 ERA) for Arizona and Matthew Boyd (7.00 ERA) for Chicago, have demonstrated significant struggles this season. Compounding this, strong winds exceeding 20 mph are forecast to be blowing out at Wrigley Field, which historically boosts offensive production. Given these factors, a high-scoring game is highly anticipated.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers @ St. Louis Cardinals
picks.rationale: While Dodgers starter Justin Wrobleski has an excellent 1.48 ERA, Cardinals pitcher Dustin May's 5.28 ERA, 1.53 WHIP, and .385 BABIP suggest he is due for significant regression. The St. Louis bullpen further exacerbates this, ranking 26th in MLB with a 5.03 ERA. The Dodgers' offense is formidable, batting a league-best .291 on the road this season. The total line has already seen movement from 9 to 9.5, with projections indicating a strong lean towards the Over.

Seattle's starting pitcher, Luis Castillo, has struggled significantly this season, holding a 0-2 record with a 6.51 ERA and a 1.73 WHIP. In contrast, Kansas City's Kris Bubic has been solid with a 2-1 record and a 3.78 ERA. The Royals have already won the last two games of this series against the Mariners, showcasing recent momentum. Additionally, the Mariners have lost four of their last five games as home favorites and have a poor record in day games following a previous day's play.

Trea Turner is projected for 1.66 hits in today's matchup, providing an 11.4% edge against his 1.5 prop line. He has been in excellent form, exceeding this line in two of his last four games, including a recent four-hit performance. Turner demonstrates strong performance against right-handed pitching, with a .300 batting average and .444 slugging percentage, and will face Miami's right-hander Chris Paddack, who carries a .337 BABIP. These factors strongly support Turner surpassing 1.5 total hits today.

Boston's starting pitcher, Ranger Suarez, has been a reliable performer this season with a 3.09 ERA and 0.94 WHIP through 35 innings. He is matched against Houston's Cody Bolton, who has exhibited significant command issues, accumulating 8 walks in just 9.1 innings and holding a high 5.79 ERA and 1.71 WHIP. This substantial pitching mismatch at Fenway Park, coupled with the Astros' struggling 13-21 record, gives the Red Sox a strong advantage to secure a multi-run victory. The movement towards Boston's moneyline also reflects increased confidence in their performance.
